homepage.heroSubline: Harness the power of California sunshine with custom solar solutions. From La Jolla to Escondido, we design systems built for San Diego's unique climate.
aboutText: Founded by local engineers who saw the need for solar solutions tailored to coastal corrosion and inland wildfire risks, Onyx Solar brings grid independence to San Diego homes.
We combine German-engineered components with California craftsmanship, ensuring every installation withstands salt air, earthquakes, and our intense UV exposure.
As active members of the San Diego Green Building Council, we're committed to making renewable energy accessible across all neighborhoods.

valueProps.title: Coastal-Grade Materials
valueProps.description: Our racking systems use marine-grade aluminum to resist salt air corrosion in beach communities.

valueProps.title: Wildfire Hardening
valueProps.description: Specialized conduit and grounding protect inland systems from ember exposure during fire season.

valueProps.title: Historic Home Expertise
valueProps.description: We've perfected low-profile installations for 1920s cottages with delicate roof structures.

valueProps.title: SDG&E Rebate Specialists
valueProps.description: Our team handles all paperwork for local utility incentives and net metering.

homepage.ctaSubline: Get a free assessment of how much you could save with San Diego's sun.

services.name: Residential Solar Installation
services.shortDescription: Custom solar arrays designed for San Diego's diverse housing styles.
services.description:
From Spanish revival roofs in North Park to modern ranches in Scripps Ranch, we engineer each system for your home's architecture and microclimate.

Our process begins with 3D modeling that accounts for coastal salt accumulation or inland dust patterns. We specify panels with advanced UV protection to withstand our 260+ sunny days annually. For historic districts, we offer concealed mounting that preserves architectural integrity.

Every installation includes SDG&E interconnection coordination and California Title 24 compliance checks. We monitor your system's performance against local weather patterns for optimal output.
process: Step 1: Site assessment with drone imaging and shading analysis
Step 2: Custom design presentation with production estimates
Step 3: Permitting (including HOA packages where required)
Step 4: Installation with minimal disruption to your household
benefits: 20-30% average reduction in SDG&E bills
Increased home value (CA studies show 4.1% premium)
Protection against rising utility rates
Reduced carbon footprint equivalent to 6,000 miles driven annually

faq.question: How do coastal installations differ from inland?
faq.answer: We use stainless steel hardware and anti-corrosion coatings near the ocean, while inland systems get additional wildfire-rated conduit and vegetation clearance planning.

faq.question: Can solar work on my 1940s bungalow?
faq.answer: Absolutely. We've installed on hundreds of historic homes using specialized flashing and weight-distributed racking that protects original roofing materials.

services.name: Solar Battery Storage
services.shortDescription: Energy resilience for San Diego's grid challenges.
services.description:
With wildfire-related PSPS outages and rising peak rates, battery storage gives San Diegans reliable power. We specialize in systems that maximize California's Self-Generation Incentive Program (SGIP) rebates.

For coastal homes, we install marine-rated battery enclosures that prevent salt air degradation. Inland customers get fire-resistant models with automatic isolation during emergencies. All our systems integrate seamlessly with SDG&E's time-of-use billing structure.

We'll show you how to store excess solar from midday production to use during 4-9pm peak periods when rates are highest. During outages, critical circuits stay powered without generator noise or fumes.
process: Step 1: Load analysis to determine essential circuits
Step 2: SGIP rebate eligibility screening
Step 3: Battery placement planning (garage/exterior options)
Step 4: Smart integration with existing or new solar
benefits: Backup power during PSPS outages
Avoid 300% price spikes during peak hours
Tax credits covering 30% of system cost
Quiet operation unlike gas generators

faq.question: How long will batteries last during an outage?
faq.answer: A typical 10kWh system can power refrigerators, lights, and wifi for 18-24 hours. With solar recharging, you can sustain essentials indefinitely.

faq.question: Are batteries worth it without solar?
faq.answer: While possible, pairing with solar maximizes value. We can design hybrid systems that charge from both grid and panels for optimal savings.

services.name: Solar Maintenance
services.shortDescription: Protect your investment from San Diego's unique challenges.
services.description:
Our climate demands specialized solar care. Coastal systems battle salt crust that reduces efficiency by up to 15%. Inland arrays face dust storms and Santa Ana wind debris.

We offer three maintenance tiers:

1) Coastal Package: Quarterly rinse downs with deionized water to prevent mineral buildup, corrosion checks, and grounding tests for salty air conductivity.

2) Inland Package: Bi-annual panel cleaning before fire season and summer peak production, vegetation management for fire safety, and thermal scans to detect microcracks from thermal cycling.

3) Premium Monitoring: Real-time alerts on production drops compared to neighborhood benchmarks, automatic service dispatch when issues arise, and annual torque checks for earthquake resilience.
process: Step 1: System health diagnostic report
Step 2: Customized cleaning plan based on location
Step 3: Performance benchmarking against local weather data
Step 4: Priority repair scheduling if needed
benefits: Prevents 5-20% annual production loss
Extends system lifespan by 3-5 years
Identifies warranty issues before expiration
Peace of mind during extreme weather

faq.question: How often should beach-area systems be cleaned?
faq.answer: Every 3 months minimum. Salt accumulation becomes harder to remove over time and can void some manufacturers' warranties.

faq.question: Do you service other companies' installations?
faq.answer: Yes, we maintain all makes and models. Our technicians are certified across 14 manufacturers common in San Diego.

services.name: EV Charger Installation
services.shortDescription: Charge your EV with sunshine using San Diego-made electricity.
services.description:
With 15% of San Diegans now driving EVs and SDG&E offering special charging rates, solar-powered charging is the smartest way to fuel up. We design systems that sync with your driving patterns and solar production.

For condo owners, we navigate complex HOA approvals using California's Right to Charge law. Homeowners get load calculations to ensure your panel can handle both air conditioning and EV charging during heat waves.

Our installs include ChargePoint or Enel X JuiceBox smart chargers that can:
- Schedule charging during solar production hours
- Balance loads with home energy use
- Qualify for $500+ in local rebates
- Provide usage reports for tax deduction documentation
process: Step 1: Driving habit analysis and electrical assessment
Step 2: Charger selection based on vehicle compatibility
Step 3: Permitting (including HOA packages if needed)
Step 4: Installation with solar integration programming
benefits: Equivalent to $1/gallon gasoline costs
Charge overnight without drawing from the grid
Future-proof 48-amp capacity for coming EV models
Increases home value by $3,000-$5,000

faq.question: Can I charge my EV during a blackout?
faq.answer: When paired with battery storage, yes. Otherwise standard systems shut off during outages for safety. We can design emergency charging solutions.

faq.question: How long until the system pays for itself?
faq.answer: With solar offset and SDG&E's EV-TOU-5 rate plan, most installations break even in 2-3 years compared to gas costs.

services.name: Solar Roof Replacement
services.shortDescription: Seamless roof and solar upgrades for aging San Diego homes.
services.description:
Many San Diego roofs installed in the 1980s are due for replacement—a perfect opportunity to integrate solar. We coordinate entire roof+solar projects with single-point responsibility.

For tile roofs common in Mission Hills and Clairemont, we use specialized mounting that preserves waterproofing. Composition shingle replacements in El Cajon get integrated solar shingle options. All work meets California's strict fire codes for roof assemblies.

Our process includes:
- Full tear-off and deck inspection
- Ice and water shield for rare rain events
- New roof warranty that covers solar penetrations
- Solar reinstallation with zero landfill waste (we recycle all old materials)
- Handling all SDG&E paperwork for system reactivation
process: Step 1: Comprehensive roof evaluation with moisture scans
Step 2: Material selection based on neighborhood aesthetics
Step 3: Solar removal and safe component storage
Step 4: Coordinated roofing and solar reinstallation
benefits: 25% tax credit applies to roof costs when solar is added
Single contractor accountability
No duplicate labor costs for solar removal/reinstall
Improved energy efficiency from proper attic ventilation

faq.question: Can you reuse my existing solar panels?
faq.answer: In 90% of cases, yes. We test all components and only replace what's necessary. Most systems can be reinstalled with updated racking.

faq.question: How long does the process take?
faq.answer: Typical 3-bedroom homes complete in 5-7 days. We time roofing work during predictable weather and protect interiors with full containment.

reviews.name: Mirabel T.
reviews.text: The Onyx team designed a perfect system for our 1936 Craftsman in North Park. They custom-matched the racking to our original clay tiles and even helped navigate the historic district approval process. Our first SDG&E bill was $18 - down from $280!

reviews.name: Rafael G.
reviews.text: When the power went out during last October's fire warnings, our Enphase batteries kept the lights on for 36 hours straight. The installation crew explained everything in Spanish for my parents and set up the app so we can track savings.

reviews.name: Sophia K.
reviews.text: As a Tesla owner in a Pacific Beach condo, I thought HOA rules would block a charger. Onyx handled all the paperwork and installed a sleek unit that charges entirely from my balcony solar panels. Now my neighbors are asking for referrals!

reviews.name: Connor B.
reviews.text: Living two blocks from the ocean in La Jolla, I didn't realize salt buildup had dropped my system's output by 18%. Their coastal cleaning package restored full production, and now they automatically schedule cleanings around marine layer season.

reviews.name: Lupita R.
reviews.text: Our 1980s Escondido home needed a new roof and solar upgrade. Onyx managed everything - stored our panels safely during reroofing, upgraded to more efficient models, and maximized the tax credits. The project finished three days early despite February rains.

reviews.name: Darius W.
reviews.text: In Scripps Ranch, we needed wildfire-resistant components. Onyx used special conduit and designed the array with defensible space in mind. Their monitoring caught a squirrel-chewed wire before it became a hazard - above and beyond service.

service-areas.title: San Diego Solar Service Areas | Onyx Solar
service-areas.description: Serving Chula Vista, Carlsbad, Escondido and all San Diego County with climate-specific solar solutions.

serviceAreas.content: Onyx Solar proudly serves Chula Vista, a vibrant suburban community with thousands of 1970s-80s era homes that are perfect for solar panel installations. With its mild year-round climate, Chula Vista residents enjoy abundant sunshine, making solar energy a smart investment for reducing electricity bills. Our customized solar solutions help homeowners take advantage of the city's solar-friendly policies and incentives while preserving the character of their neighborhoods.
serviceAreas.localContext: Chula Vista's suburban homes from the 1970s-80s often have spacious rooftops ideal for solar arrays.

serviceAreas.content: In Carlsbad, Onyx Solar specializes in providing energy solutions for beach community cottages that benefit from the city's mild, sunny climate. Whether you own a charming coastal home or a modern residence, our solar installations are designed to withstand the salty ocean air while maximizing energy production. Carlsbad's commitment to sustainability aligns perfectly with our mission to deliver clean, reliable solar power to every home.
serviceAreas.localContext: Carlsbad's beachfront properties and sunny climate make solar an excellent choice for energy savings.

serviceAreas.content: Escondido's 1960s-70s ranch-style homes are well-suited for solar energy upgrades, and Onyx Solar is here to help. The city's mild climate ensures consistent sunlight, allowing homeowners to generate ample clean energy year-round. Our team understands the unique architectural styles of Escondido and designs solar systems that blend seamlessly with these classic homes while boosting efficiency.
serviceAreas.localContext: Escondido's ranch-style homes often feature large, south-facing roofs ideal for solar panels.

serviceAreas.content: As the largest city in the region, San Diego offers a diverse range of homes and businesses that can benefit from Onyx Solar's expertise. From historic neighborhoods to modern developments, our solar solutions are tailored to meet the needs of every property. San Diego's sunny climate and progressive energy policies make it a leader in renewable energy adoption.
serviceAreas.localContext: San Diego's year-round sunshine and eco-conscious community make solar a popular choice.

serviceAreas.content: Onyx Solar helps Oceanside residents harness the power of the sun in this coastal city known for its stunning beaches and mild climate. Whether you live in a beach cottage or a suburban home, our solar systems are built to endure coastal conditions while delivering maximum energy savings. Oceanside's commitment to sustainability makes it an ideal location for solar energy.
serviceAreas.localContext: Oceanside's coastal homes benefit from solar energy to offset higher cooling costs in summer.

serviceAreas.content: Encinitas is a solar-friendly community where Onyx Solar provides tailored energy solutions for its eclectic mix of homes. The city's sunny weather and ocean breezes create perfect conditions for solar panel efficiency. Whether you own a historic bungalow or a modern estate, our team ensures your solar installation meets both aesthetic and performance standards.
serviceAreas.localContext: Encinitas' blend of coastal charm and sunshine makes solar a practical investment.

serviceAreas.content: Vista's mix of suburban homes and open spaces makes it a great candidate for solar energy solutions from Onyx Solar. With mild temperatures year-round, homeowners can rely on consistent solar production to power their households. Our team designs systems that cater to Vista's unique landscape, ensuring optimal performance and energy savings.
serviceAreas.localContext: Vista's suburban homes often feature ample roof space for efficient solar panel placement.

serviceAreas.content: La Mesa's charming neighborhoods and sunny climate make it a prime location for Onyx Solar's services. Known as the 'Jewel of the Hills,' this city benefits from abundant sunshine, allowing homeowners to maximize their solar energy production. Our installations are designed to complement La Mesa's architectural styles while delivering long-term savings.
serviceAreas.localContext: La Mesa's hilly terrain offers elevated solar exposure for many homes.

serviceAreas.content: Poway's spacious properties and sunny climate create excellent opportunities for solar energy adoption with Onyx Solar. The city's upscale homes and rural estates often have large rooftops perfect for solar arrays. Poway's commitment to sustainability aligns with our mission to provide clean, efficient energy solutions for every household.
serviceAreas.localContext: Poway's expansive lots and sunny weather make it ideal for residential solar.

serviceAreas.content: Coronado's pristine beachfront homes and historic properties can greatly benefit from Onyx Solar's expert installations. The island's mild climate and ample sunshine ensure high solar energy production year-round. Our corrosion-resistant solar panels are perfect for coastal environments, helping Coronado residents achieve energy independence.
serviceAreas.localContext: Coronado's coastal location requires durable solar solutions built to withstand salty air.
